title: Implement six exact typed stage contracts
description:
## Context

CAP-D2 implements accepted decision D2 after the trusted catalog exists. The runtime needs distinct bounded request/result contracts for Questions, Research, Design, Structure, Plan, and Implementation rather than one stringly typed stage shape.

## Scope

Define the six built-in StageContract implementations, their Effect Schemas, exact authority-ordered source envelope, task construction, prepared-output projection, compatibility checks, and deterministic registration. Read artifact sources by exact commit/path/blob identity, verify bytes and hashes, enforce individual and total request bounds, reject duplicates or reordering, and persist exact typed request identity for replay. Preserve distinct document and implementation shapes.

## Out of Scope

Agent execution; publication; review or gate lifecycles; Provenance mutation; Plan execution; Implementation execution; mutable latest-path discovery; aggregate capacity guarantees.
design: Use StageCatalog as the single heterogeneous resolution seam. Restore each concrete request/result type with its local Schema. Reuse the existing source resolver and repository adapter patterns, extending them to immutable Git artifact references. Keep common envelopes shared without forcing the six contracts through one false payload shape.
acceptance_criteria:
- All six built-in contracts resolve in deterministic order and decode distinct bounded request/result shapes.
- Requests bind WorkflowId, Generation, stage identity, exact ordered source bytes/hashes, repository target, and revision intent where applicable.
- Changed, reordered, duplicate, malformed, missing, or oversized sources and results are rejected at Schema or persistence boundaries.
- Ticket and accepted-artifact precedence is deterministic and covered by tests.
- A test contract proves extension without stage-specific orchestrator changes.
- Individual limits are tested without claiming aggregate storage capacity.
notes: Authority: CAP-D2 / wvs34-d4-bac9e02e-res-d2. Depends on CAP-D1. Likely surfaces: src/qrspi/contracts/, src/qrspi/stage-catalog.ts, src/qrspi/source-assembly.ts, src/qrspi/source-resolver.ts, src/qrspi/store.ts, src/store/migrations.ts, test/qrspi/contracts.test.ts, test/qrspi/source-assembly.test.ts. Design discussion accepted through explicit human auto-approval: .humanlayer/tasks/workflowd-vs3.4.2-implement-six-exact-typed-stage-contracts/03-design-discussion-exact-stage-contracts.md (SHA-256 5bdedf4f16c47cd8dd9bc3c62410b58c2124c36b3e87c47df67244fa7fb64ae1). It resolves six stage-tagged local Schemas over one ordered exact-source envelope, immutable commit/path/blob/content reads, catalog-contained executable erasure, canonical StageProduce request replay identity, layered per-record bounds, explicit built-in registration order, and registration-only extension tests. Bead intentionally remains in progress; no implementation, commit, push, PR, close, or Dolt remote sync performed.

dependencies.title: Build trusted stage definitions and catalog
dependencies.description:
## Context

CAP-D1 implements accepted decision D1 on the pinned Structure baseline 42e129ab. WorkflowStart already supplies WorkflowDefinition Schemas, normalization, hashing, and definition persistence, but no trusted StageCatalog or complete contract/harness availability seam exists.

## Scope

Add the server-owned StageCatalog and stable contract/harness reference interfaces; validate registration identity, compatibility, availability, deterministic ordering, activation prerequisites, safe artifact paths, and retained versions before work becomes claimable. Compose the catalog through Effect Layer and extend the existing definition persistence only where executable snapshots or registration identity are missing.

## Out of Scope

Repository-loaded plugins, prompts or Schemas; per-stage Context tags or workers; owner lifecycles; service status/readiness; aggregate capacity policy.
dependencies.design: Reuse WorkflowStageDefinition, WorkflowDefinition, normalizeWorkflowDefinition, workflowDefinitionSha256, existing config loading, and qrspi_workflow_definitions from WorkflowStart. Keep type erasure inside StageCatalog resolution and restore concrete types by Schema decoding. Use stable semantic names with version represented as data.
dependencies.acceptance_criteria:
- Every enabled StageDefinition resolves one trusted compatible contract and harness before any operation becomes claimable.
- Unknown, duplicate, unavailable, incompatible, unsafe, or hash-mismatched definitions fail with exact diagnostics.
- Active referenced versions remain resolvable across restart.
- A test built-in registration can be added without a new worker, queue, store family, Context tag, or central stage-kind switch.
- Layer and configuration tests prove V1 boundaries while making no aggregate-capacity claim.
dependencies.notes:
Authority: CAP-D1 / wvs34-d4-bac9e02e-res-d1. Canonical Structure SHA a89aa5cf. Likely surfaces: src/qrspi/catalog.ts, src/qrspi/domain.ts, src/qrspi/ports.ts, src/config.ts, src/layers.ts, src/qrspi/store.ts, src/store/migrations.ts, test/qrspi/catalog.test.ts, test/layers.test.ts. Research-question stage completed: .humanlayer/tasks/workflowd-vs3.4.1-build-trusted-stage-definitions-and-catalog/01-research-questions-stage-catalog.md. Codebase research stage completed: .humanlayer/tasks/workflowd-vs3.4.1-build-trusted-stage-definitions-and-catalog/02-research-stage-definitions-catalog.md. Current-state map covers definition decoding/validation/hashing/persistence, ordered stage semantics, TrustedAgentHarnessCatalog, retry/restart retention, Effect Layer composition, tests, and documented extension boundaries. No open research questions. Design discussion accepted through explicit human auto-approval: .humanlayer/tasks/workflowd-vs3.4.1-build-trusted-stage-definitions-and-catalog/03-design-discussion-trusted-stage-catalog.md (SHA-256 9bd58c5f6aa7a4db5dcb68cc9668720c843f471ce8803813b8e40dd40fc27666). It resolves the stage shape, catalog erasure seam, registration hashes, pre-Generation validation, minimal stage snapshot persistence, typed diagnostics, Effect composition, and test boundary. Bead intentionally remains in progress; no implementation, commit, push, PR, close, or Dolt remote sync performed.
Structure outline accepted through explicit human auto-approval: .humanlayer/tasks/workflowd-vs3.4.1-build-trusted-stage-definitions-and-catalog/04-structure-outline-trusted-stage-catalog.md (SHA-256 578072f84a8408003f362fe98ccf8e67af59587de99b350772fa2c90268a17cc). It defines four vertical phases: trusted single-stage Generation creation, deterministic complete-definition validation, restart identity fencing, and QRSPI-local fail-closed composition with registration-only extension proof. Recursive scope review was not run because docs/research/design-scope-review.md does not exist; no SplitFeature or PromoteToEpic result and no child Beads were created. Stopped after Structure acceptance. No Plan, implementation, commit, push, PR, Bead close, or Dolt remote sync performed.
Implementation plan accepted through explicit human auto-approval: .humanlayer/tasks/workflowd-vs3.4.1-build-trusted-stage-definitions-and-catalog/05-plan-trusted-stage-catalog.md (SHA-256 65242e0f23ad86abf16a64c828c71decd16582e1c9191ffb64da6c4874101bea). It details four independently testable phases: trusted single-stage resolution and atomic snapshot persistence, deterministic complete-definition validation, restart identity fencing, and QRSPI-local fail-closed composition with registration-only extension proof. No implementation, commit, push, PR, Bead close, or Dolt remote sync performed.
Implementation completed through all four accepted plan phases with local commits ba0a4d9, a356b82, 117e1d3, and 893a535. Final verification: bun run check passed (563 tests; typecheck; 105/105 Effect diagnostics; skill sync; lint; formatting; coverage). Final review found no remaining accepted-plan gaps or prohibited scope. Bead intentionally remains in progress pending human review; no push, PR, close, or Dolt remote sync performed.
dependencies.close_reason: Implementation merged to origin/main in PR #16 at c5a18e27c709facd9bd21c991fccea720a410229

dependencies.title: Run configurable QRSPI stages and publish their artifacts
dependencies.description:
## Context
WorkflowStart establishes the current Generation and ticket branch. Workflowd then needs a built-in but extensible stage model that can add future kinds of agent-built work without coupling stage meaning to the current OpenCode execution method.

The QRSPI contract separates serializable StageDefinition policy, typed stage request and result contracts, durable WorkflowOperation execution, immutable artifact publication, review, and human gates. QRSPI uses the ticket branch directly and creates no pull request during stage work.

## Task
Implement the configured Questions, Research, Design, Structure, Plan, and Implementation stages using a trusted StageCatalog. Each serializable StageDefinition selects a versioned stage contract and agent harness plus input bounds, producer policy, artifact or checkpoint policy, automated-review policy, and human-gate policy.

Stage contracts are built into trusted Workflowd code and extensible by registering another contract implementation. Each contract owns its Effect Schema input and result plus stage-specific task construction. The generic stage runner resolves the exact contract, decodes durable input, delegates agent execution to the selected AgentHarness, validates the result, and returns prepared output to the WorkflowOperation lifecycle.

Publish document artifacts and implementation commits through separate ArtifactPublish operations using signed exact-parent commits and exact-old ref updates. Advance StageRun only from authoritative publication, review, or gate outcomes. Successor stages consume acceptedRevision only.

## Out of scope
Do not load stage code, prompts, schemas, or harnesses from a repository. Do not implement a generic plugin loader, arbitrary stage DAG, TypeScript generic chain for the whole runtime-configured workflow, or one worker and Context tag per stage. Do not create or update a pull request during QRSPI stage work.
dependencies.design:
Keep four concerns separate:

- StageDefinition is durable, serializable, hashable trusted data.
- StageContract is built-in runtime behavior with local typed input and result schemas.
- AgentHarness owns how agent work executes and may evolve independently of stage meaning.
- WorkflowOperation owns leases, fencing, retries, replacement, external effects, currentness, and recovery.

Provide one StageCatalog through Effect Layer composition. Register plain built-in contract values rather than one Context tag per stage. The heterogeneous catalog may erase types only inside its resolution seam; Schema decoding must restore the selected contract type before stage-specific code runs.

Use stable names such as StageDefinition, StageContractRef, StageContract, StageCatalog, StageRun, and StageRevision. Store contractVersion on references and include it in stage and workflow hashes. Never add version suffixes to type names. Retain referenced contract implementations while active durable work can resolve them.

Adding a built-in stage should require its contract module, trusted registration, definition policy, and tests. It should not require a new queue, worker loop, store branch, or generic orchestrator conditional. Changing the selected harness should not change stage result semantics.

Do not force DocumentStageRevision and ImplementationStageRevision through one false execution shape. They share StageRun progression and WorkflowOperation infrastructure but retain their different artifact and checkpoint contracts.

Use paths and publication rules from docs/qrspi-contract.md. Later accepted technical artifacts refine earlier technical detail, while the Ticket always outranks them for product meaning.
dependencies.acceptance_criteria:
- Workflowd loads a server-owned ordered WorkflowDefinition and validates every StageDefinition, stage contract reference, harness reference, policy, and hash before a Generation can use it.
- Questions, Research, Design, Structure, Plan, and Implementation are registered built-in contracts; configured stages run in deterministic order and disabled stages create no run.
- Adding a test built-in stage requires registration and contract code but no new queue, worker loop, store method family, or central stage-kind switch.
- Stage and harness references use stable semantic type names with version represented only as data.
- Unknown, duplicate, unavailable, or incompatible contract versions fail before work becomes claimable; referenced active versions remain resolvable across restart.
- Each stage input is bounded, Schema-decoded, tied to exact accepted source references, and assembled according to ticket and artifact precedence.
- Agent execution uses the selected AgentHarness without giving the harness authority to advance StageRun or publish Git state.
- Document publication uses one signed exact-parent commit and exact-old remote update; duplicate or uncertain effects recover through authoritative observation.
- Successor stages consume only acceptedRevision, and stale Generation, revision, session, review, or publication outcomes cannot advance the workflow.
- No pull request is created or updated during QRSPI stage work.
- Tests cover registration, extension with another built-in contract, skip, success, retry, restart, stale Generation, contract-version recovery, uncertain publication, document revision, and implementation checkpoint handoff.
dependencies.notes:
STRUCTURE APPROVED: Canonical Structure .humanlayer/tasks/workflowd-vs3.4-configurable-qrspi-stages/04-structure-canonical-r1.md SHA a89aa5cfc2295ab9f1c660d897bf9c815a13b845fd3585af52123f6c7030c9fc was human-approved by gate response 04-structure-gate-response-r1.json SHA afbe415e82927954607b4f5a3b955d42851ca985e0b4c887d029dd7ddd73854c. It derives CAP-D1 through CAP-D12 from accepted decisions D1-D12, keeps D13 prohibition-only, preserves R9 under workflowd-8bg, and routes all capabilities through split flow. Split analysis was rerun against accepted main baseline 42e129ab (not the older current root branch) using four disjoint repository-reading agents. Delivery children freeze concrete module/schema/interface/transaction/fixture boundaries without changed-line thresholds. External review/gate/Provenance/route lifecycles remain in workflowd-vs3.5/.6/.9/.14.
SPLIT FLOW LANDED: Created implementation-ready child tasks workflowd-vs3.4.1 through workflowd-vs3.4.12, one per accepted CAP-D1 through CAP-D12. Each child records concrete scope, design seam, exclusions, verification outcomes, likely surfaces, exact capability authority, and accepted dependencies. Corrected an initial Beads --deps direction mistake before handoff; final validation reports no dependency cycles, bd lint total 0, and the expected initial ready frontier contains only workflowd-vs3.4.1. CAP-D11 becomes parallel-ready with CAP-D4 after D3; later readiness follows the approved graph. No implementation, commit, push, or PR was started.
